standing: born as cairn/rack — mislabeled: a 'rack' that was actually a trigger-firing scheduler
re-homed as a SchedulerService under a service-abstraction framing

design conversation corrected the model twice — the rack is the CHASSIS (bus+shims); THIS is the SYSTEM DEVICE (owner of host-resource predicates), NOT a scheduler. SchedulerService + the interval/date/quantity/state trigger enum DELETED
reworked to the system-device shape and PROVEN — advertise→subscribe→poke with Law-6 local evaluation, through the heartbeat + bus

flake found + fixed (learning specimen, Law 8): the Law-6 leak-check scanned the WHOLE envelope incl. the bus-assigned uuid, which carries '95' ~12% by chance → a red decided by a coin toss; scoped to device-authored fields, re-proven 0 red / 96 concurrent runs

diagnostic PRODUCER added — subscribe() emits a thin gate-contact breadcrumb (pointer=sub_id), crawled in situ by diagnostic_interpreter: the complete-first-pass method measured in situ (Law 3). Emit additive; capstone + interpreter proofs stay green
